Title: Evolution of linear triterpenoid biosynthesis within the Euphorbia genus

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl-CoA reductase; mevalonate pathway enzyme; truncated form
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases precursor supply and strongly enhances peplusol accumulation when co-expressed with peplusol synthases in heterologous hosts.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Truncated A. thaliana HMGR increased FPP precursor supply in N. benthamiana. 2. Co-expression with EpSS-L1 increased peplusol production 35-fold to >2.5% leaf dry weight. 3. Co-expression with ElSS-L1 increased peplusol production 20-fold to 1.4% leaf dry weight.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; metabolite profiling by LC-MS and GC-MS
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV-35S constitutive promoter in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-boosting module for metabolic engineering of linear triterpenoids in plant hosts.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as an enabling background gene rather than the main discovery target; also a truncated S. cerevisiae HMG1 background increased peplusol yield in yeast.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May broadly alter mevalonate-pathway flux and endogenous triterpenoid/sterol pools.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: ER-associated enzyme (native HMGR); truncated form used for deregulated activity
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: N. benthamiana leaves; S. cerevisiae cell pellets/cultures
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Peplusol has reported antifungal activity and potential crop-care, skin-care and pharmaceutical uses; squalene is used in cosmetics and vaccine adjuvants.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Euphorbia peplus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Euphorbia peplus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Squalene synthase-like prenyltransferase; divalent metal-ion-dependent triterpene synthase with conserved DXXED motifs
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Linear triterpenoid biosynthesis from farnesyl diphosphate via head-to-head condensation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Peplusol branch of linear triterpenoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Peplusol; minor squalene under some conditions
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes head-to-head condensation of two FPP molecules to form peplusol, likely via a peplusol diphosphate intermediate and hydrolysis rather than canonical squalene reduction.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Moderate antifungal activity; potential crop-care, skin-care and pharmaceutical applications.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Latex of Euphorbia peplus; heterologous N. benthamiana leaves; S. cerevisiae cell pellets
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: ER-associated membrane enzyme inferred from conserved C-terminal membrane anchor; truncated soluble form used in E. coli assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in latex; latex-specific expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves and S. cerevisiae cultures
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV-35S in N. benthamiana; Gal10 in S. cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: N. benthamiana NA; S. cerevisiae CEN.PK2-1C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome mining; RNA-seq expression analysis; transient expression in N. benthamiana; heterologous expression in S. cerevisiae; in vitro enzyme assay in E. coli lysate; LC-MS/GC-MS; HRMS; NMR standard comparison
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EpSS-L1 is a functional peplusol synthase identified from E. peplus. 2. Transient expression in N. benthamiana produced peplusol, and co-expression with AtHMGRt increased yield 35-fold to >2.5% leaf dry weight. 3. Expression in S. cerevisiae produced peplusol up to ~30 mg/L culture with most product retained in cell pellets. 4. In vitro assays confirmed peplusol as the main product, with minor squalene under some conditions. 5. Sequence/structure-guided mutagenesis showed key residues underlying neofunctionalization from squalene synthase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strong peplusol accumulation and reduces endogenous squalene/2,3-oxidosqualene pools in N. benthamiana, consistent with competition for FPP.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-expression with truncated HMGR/tHMG1; assay cofactors NADPH, NADP+, Mg2+; pH 6.5 or 7.5 in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: E. peplus RNA-seq dataset (accession not provided in text) used for expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for synthetic biology production of peplusol in plants or yeast; can be paired with precursor-boosting HMGR modules.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can decrease endogenous squalene and 2,3-oxidosqualene in plant hosts, indicating possible sterol-pathway competition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally named EpSS-L1; authors hereinafter refer to it as EpPS. Latex-specific expression supports natural role in latex metabolism. Six to seven amino acid positions are central to product specificity.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Euphorbia peplus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Euphorbia peplus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Squalene synthase; divalent metal-ion-dependent prenyltransferase with conserved DXXED motifs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established in this paper as rate-limiting; functions as key ancestral enzyme and precursor-forming step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes reductive head-to-head condensation of two FPP molecules to form squalene.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with peplusol branch and supplies precursor pool for sterols/oxidosqualene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: High-value commodity used in cosmetics, food and pharmaceutical industries; vaccine adjuvant component after formulation/derivatization.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Broadly in E. peplus tissues; heterologous N. benthamiana leaves and S. cerevisiae cell pellets
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: ER-associated membrane enzyme inferred from conserved C-terminal membrane anchor; truncated soluble form used in E. coli assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in latex but more ubiquitous than EpSS-L1
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in N. benthamiana leaves and S. cerevisiae cultures
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV-35S in N. benthamiana; Gal10 in S. cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: N. benthamiana NA; S. cerevisiae CEN.PK2-1C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome mining; RNA-seq expression analysis; transient expression in N. benthamiana; heterologous expression in S. cerevisiae; in vitro enzyme assay in E. coli lysate; LC-MS/GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EpSS-L2 is the duplicated paralogue retaining ancestral squalene synthase activity. 2. It did not produce peplusol in N. benthamiana or yeast. 3. It increased 2,3-oxidosqualene in N. benthamiana and strongly increased squalene in S. cerevisiae. 4. In vitro assays confirmed squalene as the major product. 5. Active-site residue swaps could partially convert this enzyme into peplusol synthase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes squalene accumulation and supports oxidosqualene formation via endogenous host metabolism; does not produce peplusol.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-expression with AtHMGRt/tHMG1 background; in vitro dependence on NADPH/NADP+ and Mg2+
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: E. peplus RNA-seq dataset (accession not provided in text) used for expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering target for sustainable squalene production and template enzyme for reprogramming toward novel linear triterpenes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Redirecting flux to squalene may alter host sterol/triterpenoid balance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors hereinafter refer to EpSS-L2 as EpSS. Served as the ancestral-function comparator for peplusol synthase evolution.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Linear triterpenoid biosynthesis from farnesyl diphosphate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of FPP-derived head-to-head condensation intermediates into peplusol.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Moderate antifungal activity; potential industrial applications.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Latex of E. lateriflora; heterologous N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified from RNA-seq of latex-containing stems
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq de novo transcriptome assembly; homology search; transient expression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS/GC-MS; HRMS; NMR-verified standard comparison
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ElSS-L1 from E. lateriflora is a functional peplusol synthase homologous to EpSS-L1. 2. It produced peplusol in N. benthamiana at levels similar to EpSS-L1. 3. Co-expression with AtHMGRt increased peplusol about 20-fold to 1.4% leaf dry weight. 4. Its expression reduced squalene and 2,3-oxidosqualene in N. benthamiana, consistent with FPP competition.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes peplusol accumulation in heterologous leaves and decreases competing host triterpene intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-expression with AtHMGRt in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative peplusol synthase for engineering peplusol production in plant chassis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely competes with endogenous sterol pathway for FPP precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors hereinafter refer to ElSS-L1 as ElPS.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes reductive dimerization of two FPP molecules to form squalene, inferred from homology and heterologous activity patterns.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for sterol metabolism and competes with peplusol branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified from RNA-seq of latex-containing stems
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq de novo transcriptome assembly; homology analysis; transient expression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ElSS-L2 did not produce peplusol in N. benthamiana. 2. It significantly increased 2,3-oxidosqualene levels in leaves. 3. Based on activity pattern and high homology to EpSS-L2, it likely encodes a squalene synthase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes squalene formation rather than peplusol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: With or without AtHMGRt in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate alternative squalene synthase from Euphorbia for precursor engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May compete with peplusol branch for FPP.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional assignment is suggested/likely rather than directly confirmed by purified in vitro assay in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established here; used as characterized positive control
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts two molecules of FPP to squalene.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for sterol metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Heterologous N. benthamiana leaves; S. cerevisiae cell pellets
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: ER-associated membrane enzyme; truncated soluble form assayed in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in N. benthamiana and S. cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV-35S in N. benthamiana; Gal10 in S. cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: N. benthamiana NA; S. cerevisiae CEN.PK2-1C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Positive-control heterologous expression in N. benthamiana and S. cerevisiae; in vitro assay in E. coli; LC-MS/GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtSS served as a validated squalene synthase control. 2. It did not produce peplusol. 3. It increased squalene in N. benthamiana and S. cerevisiae and yielded squalene in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes squalene accumulation as expected for canonical squalene synthase.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: With or without HMGR boost; in vitro cofactor conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Benchmark enzyme for squalene production and comparative engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Control/background comparator rather than the focal discovery gene, but experimentally central in Results.
